data.title: Centene subsidiary awarded Nevada medicaid contract
data.description:
• Centene (NYSE:CNC) said on Thursday that its Nevada subsidiary, SilverSummit Healthplan has been selected by the Nevada Department of Health and Human Services to provide services for its Medicaid managed care program. 
  • Subject to state approval, the five-year contract is expected to begin January 1, 2026, with a possible two-year extension. 
  • SilverSummit Healthplan has served Nevada Medicaid members since 2017 and currently serves over 100,000 members. Starting January 1, 2026, SilverSummit will continue to serve Medicaid members providing managed care services including primary care, telehealth, pharmacy, maternal and behavioral health. 
  • Ambetter from SilverSummit HealthPlan, SilverSummit's marketplace offering, was also one of three bidders selected by the Nevada Department of Health and Human Services for its marketplace public option, the Battle Born State Plan (BBSP). Effective January 1, 2026, the five-year contract includes a potential two-year extension and is expected to cover 16,000 individuals in the first year. 
  • Centene (NYSE:CNC) shares were up 0.56% in extended trading.

data.title: Paranovus Entertainment receives Nasdaq letter over non-compliance with listing rules
data.description:
• Paranovus Entertainment Technology (NASDAQ:PAVS) received a letter from the Nasdaq Stock Market on April 9 notifying that the company is not in compliance with the requirements for continued listing because it did not timely file its Form 6-K for the period ended September 30, 2024.
  • The company has 60 calendar days from the date of the deficiency letter to submit a plan to regain compliance with Nasdaq's listing rules.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NewHold Investment announces separate trading of its ordinary shares and warrants
data.description:
• NewHold Investment Corp III (NASDAQ:NHIC) said on Thursday that commencing April 17, 2025, holders of the units sold in the company's initial public offering completed on March 3, 2025, may elect to separately trade the class A ordinary shares of the company and the warrants included in such units on Nasdaq. 
  • The ordinary shares and warrants that are separated will trade on Nasdaq under the symbols “NHIC” and “NHICW,” respectively. Those units not separated will continue to trade on Nasdaq under the symbol “NHICU.”

data.title: House approves Trump's 'big' budget framework, after swaying GOP holdouts
data.description:
House Republicans approved a budget framework on Thursday after House Speaker Mike Johnson was able to convince GOP holdouts that he would seek at least $1.5T in cuts to federal programs and services. Johnson achieved the win after he halted voting on Wednesday night to hammer out a deal.

The bill includes trillions of dollars in tax breaks without the deeper cuts the holdouts were seeking, the Associated Press reported. The 216-214 vote advanced the budget plan. Two conservative Republicans, along with all Democrats, voted against it.

The next step is turning the framework into bill text. If more than a couple Republicans vote against the plan at any step in the process, the plan can come to a halt. While the Democrats are in the minority, they promised to object at every turn.

House Democratic leader Hakeem Jeffries called the Republican budget plan a "toxic scheme' that proposed the biggest cuts to Medicaid and food assistance in U.S. history, "all in service of enacting massive tax breaks to their millionaire donors, like Elon Musk" — a reference to the Tesla chairman's Department of Government Efficiency cost-cutting campaign.

Johnson was able to advance the plan after a private meeting with a group of Republicans on Wednesday night worked to discuss their options.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: CNH names Jim Nickolas as CFO, succeeding Oddone Incisa
data.description:
CNH (NYSE:CNH) named Jim Nickolas as its next chief financial officer, effective May 6. He will replace Oddone Incisa, who is leaving the company after 28 years to pursue other opportunities, the maker of farm equipment said.

Nickolas brings experience in finance and capital markets, having most recently served as CFO of Martin Marietta. He also held senior roles at Caterpillar, including CFO of its resource industries segment and head of corporate development.

Incisa will remain with the company through April to support the transition. Nickolas is expected to present at CNH’s Investor Day on May 8.

data.title: TC Energy CEO rules out sale of Canadian Mainline pipeline
data.description:
TC Energy (NYSE:TRP) CEO Francois Poirier said Thursday the company would not consider selling its Canadian Mainline natural gas pipeline, Bloomberg reported.

The pipeline, which stretches across 8,700 from energy-producing Alberta to major population centers in Ontario and Quebec while remaining entirely within Canada's borders, is a key asset for TC Energy (NYSE:TRP) in its focus on natural gas transportation and power generation.

The company last year split off its oil pipelines into a separate company and is now focused on natural gas transportation and power generation, making the Mainline one of its marquee assets and ensuring that a conversion or sale of the pipeline would not be considered, Poirier said.

"We have a very large group of natural gas shippers with whom we have contractual obligations to deliver natural gas for, in some cases, many more decades,” Poirier told Bloomberg in an interview. "Given that all of our capacity is contracted, legally speaking, we wouldn't be able to consider a conversion of some of our existing infrastructure to oil service."

data.title: Harvard Bioscience announces CFO transition
data.description:
• Harvard Bioscience (NASDAQ:HBIO) announced on Thursday the resignation of Jennifer Cote, the company’s chief financial officer and treasurer. Ms.
  • The company said Cote’s resignation will be effective upon the filing of the company’s quarterly report on Form 10-Q, which is expected to be filed on or before May 12, 2025.
  • The company has appointed Mark Frost as its interim chief financial officer and treasurer, effective upon Cote’s resignation.
  • Frost has served as a consultant to the company since January 2025. Frost previously served as the chief financial officer of Fathom.
  • HBIO -1.47% after hours to $0.38.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Arcutis CFO David Topper to retire
data.description:
• Arcutis Biotherapeutics (NASDAQ:ARQT) on Thursday announced that chief financial officer David Topper is retiring from the company.
  • Latha Vairavan, currently vice president and controller, will assume the role of CFO and join the executive team as part of a planned succession, it said.
  • Topper, who has served as the company’s CFO since April 2024, will continue to serve as CFO until the filing of the company’s first quarter 10-Q and will provide transition support through his departure on May 15, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release
